title: Dysoxylum binectariferum
taxonomy: Dysoxylum binectariferum is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. It is a type of tree found in tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Dysoxylum binectariferum is within the genus Dysoxylum, which is part of the family Meliaceae. This plant is commonly known as the Asian yellow ebony. Dysoxylum binectariferum is native to parts of Southeast Asia and India.
uses: Dysoxylum binectariferum, also known as the two-horned Dysoxylum, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its bark and leaves are said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, it has been used to treat digestive problems such as diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Dysoxylum binectariferum for medicinal use.
